name: Cartagena
summary: Cartagena is a sunny Caribbean city in Colombia, surrounded by huge stone walls built long ago to keep pirates out. Inside the walls are bright painted houses, cobbled lanes and a giant hilltop fortress.
funFacts: Cartagena's old walled city is so special that UNESCO listed it as a World Heritage Site in 1984, alongside its forts and harbour.
Spanish builders worked for almost 200 years to finish about 11 kilometres of stone walls right around the city to stop pirate attacks.
In 1586 the English pirate Sir Francis Drake sailed in, captured Cartagena and only left after the Spanish handed over a huge pile of gold.
Castillo San Felipe de Barajas is the biggest fortress Spain ever built in the Americas, with secret tunnels twisting underneath the hill.
Cartagena sits right on the Caribbean Sea, so it is hot and sunny all year and people travel by horse-drawn carriage through the old streets.
thingsToDo: Walk along the top of the old stone city walls and look out over the bright blue Caribbean Sea.
Climb the zigzag ramps of Castillo San Felipe and explore the spooky echoing tunnels underneath.
Pass through the yellow Torre del Reloj clock tower gate into the colourful old town.
Spot the chubby bronze Botero statue in leafy Plaza Santo Domingo and watch the carriages roll by.
Browse the rainbow vault shops at Las Bovedas, once dungeons, now full of crafts and souvenirs.
Hop a fast boat to the Rosario Islands to snorkel above bright coral reefs and tropical fish.

wildlife.name: Cotton-top Tamarin
wildlife.description: Tiny endangered Colombian monkey with a wild tuft of long white hair on its head.

wildlife.name: Scarlet Macaw
wildlife.description: Big rainforest parrot with bright red, yellow and blue feathers and a loud squawk.

wildlife.name: Brown-throated Sloth
wildlife.description: Sleepy three-toed tree climber that moves super slowly and sometimes looks like it is smiling.

wildlife.name: Blue Morpho Butterfly
wildlife.description: Huge butterfly whose wings flash brilliant electric blue when it flutters through the forest.

localWord.meaning: Hello in Spanish, the language everyone speaks here.

images.alt: Cartagena's walled old city beside the bright blue Caribbean Sea, with red rooftops and modern towers behind.
The huge stone fortress of Castillo San Felipe de Barajas rises on a hill above the city.
The yellow Torre del Reloj clock tower gateway into Cartagena's walled old city.
The chubby bronze Botero statue in Plaza Santo Domingo, surrounded by colourful colonial buildings.
The long row of arched vault shops at Las Bovedas, once Spanish dungeons in the city wall.
Turquoise Caribbean water around the white sandy Rosario Islands near Cartagena.
A small cotton-top tamarin monkey with a fluffy white crest of hair on its head.
A scarlet macaw with bright red, yellow and blue feathers perched on a branch.
A brown-throated three-toed sloth hangs from a tree branch with a sleepy smile.
A blue morpho butterfly with shimmering electric blue open wings.
